We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:
Ensure that the projects funded under the 20% DF are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation ready;
Direct the and the finance personnel to stop distributing the 20% as financial assistance to association without proper and adequate evaluation, monitoring and documentation provided for in COA Circular No. 2007-001. Otherwise, specifically demand from the NGO/POs the following:
1. Financial reports, audited by an independent Certified Public Accountant, for the past three years preceding the date of project implementation, to ensure that it has a stable financial condition and that the funds provided by the GO shall not be its sole source of funds. For NGO/PO which has been in operation for less than three (3) years, report of accomplishment or any equivalent proof certified by its President and Secretary that it had previously implemented similar projects shall be required, in addition to financial reports for the years it has been in operation.
WFP, and Sources and Details of Proponents Equity Participation in the Project.
Complete project proposal approved/ signed by its officers which shall include the objectives, target beneficiaries, feasibility studies, risk assessment, designs, plans, blueprints, charts, etc.
List and/or photographs of similar projects previously completed by the NGO/PO, if any, indicating the source of funds for their implementation.
A sworn affidavit of the Secretary of the NGO/PO that none of its incorporators, organizers, directors or officials is an agent of or related by consanguinity or affinity up to the fourth civil degree to the officials of the GO authorized to process and/or approve the proposal, the MOA and the release of funds. Relationship of these nature shall automatically disqualify the NGO/PO form the beginning granted the fund.
c. Apply the following procedures that shall be strictly complied with:
Identify the priority projects under its WFP which may be implemented by the NGO/PO, their purposes, specifications and intended beneficiaries as well as the time frame within which the projects are to be undertaken. Xx
For each project proposal, the Municipality shall accredit the NGO/PO partners through the BAC or a committee created for the purpose, which shall formulate the selection criteria. Xx
Upon proper evaluation, the Municipality, thru the Committee, shall award the project to the NGO/PO which meets the minimum qualification xxx. The project shall be covered by a MOA which shall embody the terms of reference such as.

observations.managementAction: Copies of the 4 contracts and supporting documents were submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management direct the concerned Department Heads and authorized personnel to comply with DBM Circular No. 10 dated March 29, 1996, COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and related Civil Service Rules to ensure that the overtime services rendered are deemed necessary and urgent, granted only to authorized personnel, and claims are properly documented.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor directed that overtime services were granted only to authorized personnel. Not all offices had submitted the lacking documents called for in the Notices of Suspension.

observations.recommendation: BAC: (a) consider the integrity and proficiency in the selection of the head of BAC Secretariat; (b) formulate and adopt mechanisms in the selection of the members of TWG and Observers; and c) prepare and submit the procurement monitoring report.
observations.managementAction: Reconstitution of BAC Secretariat was made before the year ended. The selection of the head of BAC Secretariat had already formulated and adopted a mechanism in the selection of observers and TWG.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor implement the projects identified in the Annual Budget and require the Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking supporting documentation.
observations.managementAction: Management complied on the submission of the lacking documents. Likewise, the projects identified under the Barangay Wide Development Program were already identified in the Annual Budget.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management direct the Municipal Budget Officer to maintain separate registry for QRF, MOOE and Capital Outlay for proper fund control, and the Municipal Accountant to: (a) prepare the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF; (b) observe the proper transfer of DRRM funds to Trust fund books; and, (c) full disclosure of the unexpended balance of DRRMF of prior years in the Notes.
observations.managementAction: Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of the DRRMF was made and simultaneously submitted to the Auditor on 4/20/2016. The remaining balance of DRRM funds of prior years were already transferred to Special Trust Fund. Full disclosure of the fund has already been made in the Notes.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the outstanding cash advances be liquidated immediately pursuant to Sec. 89 of P.D. 1445, COA Circ. No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 and COA Circular No. 2012-004 dated November 28, 2012. We also recommend that the Municipal Mayor disseminates to all officials and employees with unliquidated cash advances, a copy of COA Circular No. 2012-004 dated November 28, 2012 and have them acknowledge the receipt of the circular to serve as a final demand to settle their unliquidated cash advance. We likewise recommend for the strict enforcement of the pertinent sanctions provided for in COA Circular No. 2012-004 dated November 28, 2012, including the suspension of salaries, if the demand remains unheeded. These recommendations are without prejudice to the issuance of Notices of Suspension and/or Disallowance resulting from post-audit of transactions.
observations.managementAction: Some employees had already submitted their liquidation which were already recorded in the books. We also recommend that the Municipal Mayor disseminates to all officials and employees with unliquidated cash advance a copy of COA Circular No. 2012-004 dated November 28, 2012 and have them acknowledge the receipt of the circular to serve as a final demand to settle their unliquidated cash advance.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management strictly comply with the requirements of COA Circular No. 75-6, dated November 7, 1975 and Section 492) of P.D. 1445 in order to control and regulate the use of government vehicles, and to ensure that the fuel consumed is for government use. Likewise, require the drivers concerned to submit duly approved Driver’s Trip Tickets with the Monthly Report of Official Travels, and Monthly Report of Fuel consumption. This recommendation is without prejudice to the issuance of Notice of Suspensions/Disallowance as a result of post-audit.
observations.managementAction: Some employees already attached duly accomplished Driver’s Trip Tickets. Reports of Fuel Consumption were still not prepared and submitted. Likewise, require the drivers concerned to submit duly approved Driver’s Trip Tickets with the Monthly Report of Official Travels, and Monthly Report of Fuel consumption.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to
provide additional information on the notes to the financial statements and
strictly observe the proper recording of transactions in accordance with the
PPSAS.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to
provide depreciation charges on the accounts Parks, Plazas and Monuments and
Work/Zoo Animals in accordance with PPSAS 17 so that the accounts will be
fairly presented in the financial statements.

We recommended that the BAC Secretariat be required to submit the
contracts and the related supporting documents of the two (2) Infrastructure
contracts for auditorial review. Likewise, require the Municipal Accountant to
submit for audit the twenty-seven (27) DVs with an aggregate amount of
P1,581,380.97 and the lacking supporting documents for expenses amounting to
P4,072,621.54.

We recommended that Management strictly observe the updated
guidelines on the appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund as
prescribed in DILG/DBM JMC No. 2017-01 dated February 22, 2017 and
require the Municipal Accountant to see to it that all claims for final payment of
infrastructure projects are supported with the as-built plans and warranty
certificates.

We recommended that Management:
a) Strictly abide with the provisions of DILG Memorandum Circular
No. 2012-73 and refrain from charging expenses not related to
disaster risk reduction management activities.
b) Refrain from charging expenses against QRF without resolution and
declaration of state of calamity.
c) Instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to include the budget for the
administrative expenses of the LDRRMO in the regular budget and
prepare a separate budget for the 5% LDRRMF in accordance with
Section 5.1.1 of COA Circular 2012-002;
d) Require the LDRRMO to include in the LDRRMFIP the projects and
activities to be funded out of unexpended balances of the LDRRMF of
the previous years;
e) Require the Municipal Budget Officer to maintain separate registries
for the LDRRMF-MOOE and Capital Outlay for the current year
and for the continuing appropriations of the previous years; and
f) Require the Municipal Accountant to determine the unexpended
balances of MOOE 70% as well as the 30% QRF for CYs 2014 to
2017 and facilitate the transfer of the balances to the special trust
fund.

We recommended that the Local School Board (LSB) prepare and
approve an annual budget for the ensuing years as provided under Sections 99
and 100(c) RA 7160 and to follow the guidelines on the preparation of the SEF
budget as provided for in DEPED-DBM-DILG Joint Circular (JC) No. 1, s.
2017.

We recommended that the LGU establish the LCPC and utilize the funds
allocated for the implementation of programs, projects and activities as required
in Section 15 of Republic Act 9344 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-
120 dated July 4, 2012.

We recommended that Management strictly comply with Sections 22 and
23 of the Omnibus Rules on Leave on the grant of monetization of leave
credits.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant draw a journal entry voucher to record the donated equipment to its proper asset account and provide adequate disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements. Moreover, require the Property and Supply unit to record the other in-kind donations in the Registry of Donated Relief Goods for DRRM and prepare monthly reports on the receipt and utilization of the in-kind donations. Henceforth, adhere strictly to COA Circulars 2012-002 and 2014-002 on the accounting and reporting of the LDRRMF.
observations.managementAction: AAR already booked-up. However, the monthly reports on the receipt and utilization of the in-kind donations were not submitted to the audit team.
observations.reason: On-going preparation of the report on the utilization of the in-kind donations.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the GSO and the Municipal Accountant be required to prepare and submit the SSMI together with the RISs to the Auditor to support the reported issuances of inventories. Likewise, require the Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of inventories, prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Inventories and submit the same to the auditor.
observations.managementAction: AAR already prepared the SSMI No. 2. However, copies of the SSMI were still not submitted to the audit team. The Inventory Committee had not conducted physical count of Inventories.
observations.reason: On-going preparation of the SSMI. The Inventory Committee had not conducted physical count of Inventories. Report on the Physical count of Inventories were not also submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management strictly observe the guidelines on the implementation of the RAY-DILG funded subprojects in accordance with DILG Memorandum Circular No. 150 Series of 2013 and RA 9184 and its IRR.
